records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Humanized (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Humanizing (?).]

defs: To render human or humane; to soften; to make gentle by overcoming cruel dispositions and rude habits; to refine or civilize.
quotes: Was it the business of magic to humanize our natures with compassion?

defs: To give a human character or expression to.
To convert into something human or belonging to man; as, to humanize vaccine lymph.
To become or be made more humane; to become civilized; to be ameliorated.
quotes: By the original law of nations, war and extirpation were the punishment of injury. Humanizing by degrees, it admitted slavery instead of death; a further step was the exchange of prisoners instead of slavery.
